citations.citation_text: Cleveland v. McCruel, 8th Dist. Cuyahoga No. 103971, 2017-Ohio-182.
citations.proposition: An order placing a defendant in a statutory pretrial-diversion program is immediately appealable when it affects the state's substantial right to complete the prosecution.

citations.citation_text: State v. Slater, 8th Dist. Cuyahoga No. 101358, 2014-Ohio-5552.
citations.proposition: A presentence plea-withdrawal request may be denied when a knowing and voluntary plea is followed only by a change of heart.

citations.citation_text: State v. Torres, 8th Dist. Cuyahoga No. 111037, 2022-Ohio-3230.
citations.proposition: Newly discovered evidence warrants a new trial only when it creates a strong probability of changing the result and satisfies the remaining Petro factors.

citations.citation_text: State v. Harrowfield, 8th Dist. Cuyahoga No. 113947, 2024-Ohio-4871.
citations.proposition: Police must obtain a second warrant before viewing any cloud backup synchronized after a phone is seized.

citations.citation_text: State v. Wachee, 8th Dist. Cuyahoga No. 110117, 2021-Ohio-4427.
citations.proposition: An App.R. 26(B) applicant must support proposed errors with record-based facts showing a genuine, colorable claim of ineffective appellate assistance.

citations.citation_text: State v. Burton, 8th Dist. Cuyahoga No. 107054, 2019-Ohio-2431.
citations.proposition: Counsel's failure to file a suppression motion is not automatically deficient; the record must show that the motion had a reasonable probability of success.

citations.citation_text: State v. Mercer-Lane, 8th Dist. Cuyahoga No. 112608, 2023-Ohio-4412.
citations.proposition: Kidnapping and a sexual offense merge categorically whenever they occur during the same encounter.

citations.citation_text: State v. Radney, 8th Dist. Cuyahoga No. 103869, 2016-Ohio-5328.
citations.proposition: Kidnapping under R.C. 2905.01(A)(4) is mitigated to a second-degree felony when the accused proves the victim was released in a safe place unharmed.

citations.citation_text: State v. Delvallie, 8th Dist. Cuyahoga No. 109315, 2022-Ohio-470.
citations.proposition: A defendant must prove beyond a reasonable doubt that the Reagan Tokes statutory framework violates due process or the separation-of-powers doctrine.

citations.citation_text: State v. Anderson, 8th Dist. Cuyahoga No. 112514, 2024-Ohio-843.
citations.proposition: Consecutive sentences are contrary to law when the trial court fails to make the proportionality finding required by R.C. 2929.14(C)(4).

citations.citation_text: State v. Burton, 8th Dist. Cuyahoga No. 114052, 2025-Ohio-650.
citations.proposition: When postrelease-control notice was correctly given at the hearing but misstated in the journal entry, a nunc pro tunc entry may correct the clerical error.

citations.citation_text: State v. Bonnell, 8th Dist. Cuyahoga No. 96368, 2011-Ohio-5837.
citations.proposition: Ohio recognizes entrapment whenever a confidential informant initiates contact with the accused.
